comprehension_gate.instruction:
READ THIS ENTIRE PROMPT before posting your comprehension gate. Then READ every file listed in required_attachments. Then POST a comprehension gate comment on Vikunja Task 121, prefixed:
[agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension]
Your comprehension comment MUST contain ALL of the following sections (verbatim section headers required, in this exact order, no numbered prefixes):
A. MILESTONE OBJECTIVE (3-5 sentences in your own words) B. WORK ITEMS (one sentence for each WI-1..WI-2 — do NOT group) C. FILES TO CREATE (list every new file with exact path — should be exactly TWO: docs/governance/README.md and the ledger entry) D. FILES TO MODIFY (should be empty — this EA creates only) E. FILES TO READ (every governance doc + STYLE.md + Sprint 9 SDV + the two deferred tickets' bodies + this prompt) F. NEGATIVE CONSTRAINTS YOU UNDERSTAND (enumerate NC-1..NC-8 from §5 in your own words — MUST list every NC individually) G. OPEN QUESTIONS (any ambiguity requiring SDO clarification) H. STRUCTURAL RECITATION (§ 7 line-by-line — verbatim section-header outline of the README's sections, in document order) I. DOC INVENTORY (enumerate every file in docs/governance/ as of parent HEAD, with one-line abstract per file, to confirm you have read them)
If you cannot produce every section with substantive content, STOP and ask in the comprehension gate comment rather than guessing.
comprehension_gate.stop_condition: After posting the comprehension gate, STOP. Do not touch any file, do not branch, do not create docs. Wait for SDO to post [agent:sdo][phase:comprehension-review] VERDICT: APPROVED. If SDO returns REVISE, address feedback and re-post the gate (same prefix).

milestone.objective: Author the governance landing page at docs/governance/README.md: a synthesis document that indexes every doc now present in docs/governance/, flags the two deferred domains (GOV-01 credential lifecycle, GOV-10 weight integrity) with their ISS-4 Pluton-blocked rationale, flags the phantom forward-reference (boot-sequence.md / GOV-15), notes the pending TEST_GOVERNANCE.md migration (GOV-MIGRATE), and provides an audience- taxonomy matrix showing which docs serve which of the five personas. The landing page is synthesis-only: it adds no new governance content, only navigation and index structure. It MUST conform to STYLE.md's Doc Template conventions (adapted for an index doc — see WI-1 structure), clear the 150-line substantive floor, and cite STYLE.md as the authority for the governance class. Scope held strictly to docs/governance/README.md plus one ledger entry; zero prior governance doc edited (L-18); zero production code modified (L-15).
milestone.sprint_context: Sprint 9 governance docs rollout (Task 121, DEC-15 sprint_id=9). EA-1..EA-4 authored 12 domain governance docs + STYLE.md. An additional fleet-hygiene.md landed during the sprint from an adjacent fleet-hygiene maturation stream (commit series ending a6ba981..c2a2ca2) — this EA MUST include it in the landing-page inventory even though it was not in the original 14-domain plan. EA-5 closes the sprint by authoring the landing page. There is no EA-6.

work_item.title: docs/governance/README.md (landing page)
work_item.vikunja_subtask: none — synthesis doc, not a GOV-NN ticket
work_item.scope: New file. Index + navigation doc for the governance class. Synthesis only — contains no new normative content. Every claim the landing page makes about a domain must be backed by the linked doc.
work_item.structure:
STYLE.md-adapted layout for an index doc. Required sections IN THIS ORDER (verbatim headers):
# Governance Documentation Index ## Audience ## How to Read This Directory ## Governance Domain Inventory ### Security and Wire Protocol ### Runtime Behavior and Resilience ### Operational State ### Ops, Deployment, and Rules ### Fleet Hygiene ## Audience Taxonomy Matrix ## Deferred Domains ## Phantom and Forthcoming References ## Pending Migrations ## Style Authority ## Open Questions / Deferred Items
Level-3 subheaders INSIDE "Governance Domain Inventory" cluster the domain docs by EA-# origin. Each cluster has a one-paragraph abstract + an enumerated bullet list of the domain docs in that cluster with: - link (relative, resolves), - one-line abstract per doc (≤ 25 words), - owning EA + GOV ticket ID.
The "Audience Taxonomy Matrix" MUST be a markdown table:
| Doc | Operator | Developer | Auditor | Incident Responder | Future Agent | |-----|----------|-----------|---------|--------------------|--------------|
with a ✓ / — marking per cell indicating whether that doc's Audience section names the persona as a primary reader. The EA builds this table by grepping each doc's Audience section, NOT by re-judging.
work_item.key_topics: - Domain-by-domain index: every file in docs/governance/ as of parent HEAD, grouped by EA-# origin - Audience taxonomy matrix (source-of-truth = each doc's own Audience section — do NOT second-guess) - Deferred domains: GOV-01 (credential lifecycle) and GOV-10 (weight integrity) with one-sentence ISS-4 deferral rationale + link to the Pluton investigation ticket (Vikunja #101) - Phantom references: boot-sequence.md (GOV-15, Vikunja #124) must be listed with "(forthcoming — see GOV-15)" and NOT linked (file does not exist) - Pending migrations: TEST_GOVERNANCE.md → docs/governance/test.md under GOV-MIGRATE (Vikunja #123), deferred until Sprint 8 closes - Navigation footer: links to CLAUDE.md "Key Documents" table, the `docs/adrs/` directory, and `docs/runbooks/`
work_item.source_anchors: - docs/governance/STYLE.md — style authority for the governance class - Each governance doc's title (# line) and Audience section - docs/sprints/sprint_9/strategic_design_vision.md §5 — scope ledger - Vikunja tickets #14 (GOV-01), #23 (GOV-10), #101 (ISS-4), #123 (GOV-MIGRATE), #124 (GOV-15)
work_item.line_floor: ≥ 150 substantive lines (comments/blank lines excluded). Matrix rows count as substantive. Padding (e.g., repeating abstracts) does NOT.
work_item.acceptance: - Every file currently in docs/governance/ (at branch parent HEAD) appears in the inventory — no omissions. Count verified by LINE-FLOOR gate output. - Every GOV-NN ticket from the Sprint 9 SDV §5.1 plan is accounted for: either linked (executed) or listed in Deferred Domains with rationale - Audience Taxonomy Matrix renders as a markdown table with exactly 5 persona columns + 1 doc column - Deferred domains section cites ISS-4 (Vikunja #101) as the common blocker - Phantom reference (boot-sequence.md) marked "(forthcoming — see GOV-15)" without a resolving link - TEST_GOVERNANCE.md migration (GOV-MIGRATE, #123) noted as post-Sprint-8 work with the destination path `docs/governance/test.md` - All five audience personas addressed in the top-level Audience section (above the matrix) - STYLE.md cited as the style authority (Style Authority section) - Line floor ≥ 150 substantive lines cleared

work_item.title: Ledger entry (Q1-1 per-file convention)
work_item.required_sections: - Frontmatter (ledger_id, date, sprint_id=9, entry_type=EA, predecessor=20260423_030132_sprint9_ea4_ops-deployment-rules, branch=feature/p5-task9-ea5-governance-landing-page, merge_commit=null, disposition=COMPLETE) - ## Task 121 / EA-5: Governance Landing Page (heading) - ### Summary (3-5 sentence narrative — what you synthesized, how the matrix was built, any doc you could not classify cleanly) - ### Deliverables (table: WI | Artifact | Lines | Notes) - ### Files Changed (every file touched, with line counts — expect exactly two: README.md + this ledger entry) - ### Quality Gates (LINE-FLOOR, STYLE.md-adapted conformance, ORACLE L-15, L16-DISJOINT, L-18 prior-doc untouched check) - ### Notes / Observations (any governance doc whose Audience section was ambiguous for the matrix; fleet-hygiene.md was NOT in the original 14-plan but is included — flag for Sprint 9 SCR awareness; any phantom or deferred references that need SDO follow-up)
work_item.acceptance: - Frontmatter complete with all required keys - README.md listed in Deliverables with its final line count - All L-15 / L-16 / L-18 compliance items explicitly addressed - fleet-hygiene.md inclusion flagged in Notes - ORACLE gate output quoted verbatim

negative_constraints.nc: DO NOT modify any file under src/, services/*/src/, launcher/, shared/, tests/, or pyproject.toml. This milestone is docs-only (L-15).

DO NOT edit any prior governance doc. STYLE.md, pgov-validation.md, ipc-protocol.md, streaming-output.md, gpu-runtime.md, error-recovery.md, circuit-breaker.md, context-spotlighting.md, session-state.md, configuration-management.md, observability.md, deployment-verification.md, rule-engine.md, and fleet-hygiene.md are ALL read-only for this EA (L-18). If a defect surfaces, record it in the ledger's Notes section for SDO triage — do NOT patch inline.
DO NOT create or stub boot-sequence.md or any other phantom-referenced doc. List boot-sequence.md in the Phantom and Forthcoming References section WITHOUT a resolving link (L-17).

DO NOT invent new normative governance claims. The landing page is an index; every claim about a domain must be a summary of (or direct quote from) the linked doc's own text. If summarizing feels too speculative, quote instead.
DO NOT create multiple ledger entries for this EA. One file only, following the Q1-1 per-file timestamp convention. DO NOT append to the frozen docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md.
DO NOT author new ADRs, DECs, decision records, or protocol specs. The landing page references existing decision records (STYLE.md, ADRs, DECs cited in prior docs) but adds none.

DO NOT second-guess each doc's Audience section when building the Audience Taxonomy Matrix. If doc X's Audience section does not name persona Y, the matrix cell is "—". If the doc uses a different label for a persona (e.g., "SRE" vs "Operator"), make a conservative mapping and record the mapping rule in the ledger's Notes section.

L-16 Sprint 8 coexistence: DO NOT touch anything under **/tests/ or anywhere else outside docs/governance/README.md + the single ledger file. Sprint 8 and Sprint 9 working sets are disjoint and must stay so.

risk.description: fleet-hygiene.md was NOT in the Sprint 9 SDV §5.1 14-doc plan; it landed from an adjacent fleet-hygiene maturation stream. The EA may either omit it (incomplete inventory) or misattribute its provenance.
risk.resolution: Include fleet-hygiene.md in the inventory under "### Fleet Hygiene" as its own cluster (single-member). Attribute provenance honestly: "Added out-of-sprint-plan from fleet-hygiene maturation stream (git log cites commits a6ba981..c2a2ca2 / 04d5e55)." Flag in ledger Notes for Sprint 9 SCR awareness. Do NOT try to backfill it into an EA-1..EA-4 cluster — it has a distinct origin.

risk.description: Audience Taxonomy Matrix construction is mechanical but error-prone — the EA may hand-judge rather than mechanically reading each doc's Audience section, producing a matrix that doesn't match ground truth.
risk.resolution: Build the matrix by opening each governance doc, reading its Audience section verbatim, and marking ✓ only where the section names the persona (case-insensitive, substring match acceptable). Record the exact matching rule in the ledger Notes. If a doc uses a near-synonym (e.g., "SRE" for "Operator", "on-call" for "Incident Responder"), apply a conservative mapping documented in Notes.

risk.description: Deferred-ticket bodies (GOV-01, GOV-10, GOV-MIGRATE, GOV-15, ISS-4) may not be read carefully, causing the landing page to state a wrong deferral rationale or blocker.
risk.resolution: Read each Vikunja ticket body (use mcp__vikunja__get_task for each: #14 GOV-01, #23 GOV-10, #101 ISS-4, #123 GOV-MIGRATE, #124 GOV-15). Quote or paraphrase the ticket body, not your memory of it. Cite the ticket ID in-doc for each deferred / phantom entry so the reader can verify independently.

risk.description: Line-floor pressure: a pure index doc may struggle to clear 150 substantive lines without padding. The Audience Taxonomy Matrix + 14 inventory entries + deferred + phantom + migration sections should get there naturally, but the risk is real.
risk.resolution: Depth comes from the per-doc abstracts (one well-chosen sentence per doc × 14 docs is ~14 lines; two-sentence abstracts are appropriate for the largest docs). The matrix is ~16 lines. If the doc comes in at 140-149 lines after substantive authoring, STOP and flag to SDO in the completion report — do NOT pad with filler.

risk.description: Parent-head drift: main may advance between authoring and EA pickup (both Sprint 8 EA-5 and fleet-infrastructure commits could land).
risk.resolution: EA MUST verify `git rev-parse HEAD` matches 232cfc9 before branching. If main has advanced, rebase onto current main. Do NOT force-checkout 232cfc9 (L-13). Predecessor ledger_id in frontmatter does not change. Re-run the inventory pass against the post-rebase `ls docs/governance/` — if new docs appeared, include them.

risk.description: The landing page's relative links may not resolve from the docs/governance/README.md location (e.g., linking with a docs/governance/ prefix instead of bare filename).
risk.resolution: README.md sits INSIDE docs/governance/, so links to sibling docs use bare filenames: `[IPC Protocol](ipc-protocol.md)` NOT `[IPC Protocol](docs/governance/ipc-protocol.md)`. Links to CLAUDE.md or runbooks use `../CLAUDE.md` or `../runbooks/`. Verify each link resolves by opening the relative path from the README's directory before committing.

ea_prompt.structural_recitation:
LANDING PAGE STRUCTURE (docs/governance/README.md):
# Governance Documentation Index ## Audience ## How to Read This Directory ## Governance Domain Inventory ### Security and Wire Protocol ### Runtime Behavior and Resilience ### Operational State ### Ops, Deployment, and Rules ### Fleet Hygiene ## Audience Taxonomy Matrix ## Deferred Domains ## Phantom and Forthcoming References ## Pending Migrations ## Style Authority ## Open Questions / Deferred Items
CLUSTER-TO-EA MAPPING (Inventory subsections): Security and Wire Protocol → EA-1 (GOV-04, GOV-02, GOV-03) Runtime Behavior and Resilience → EA-2 (GOV-05, GOV-06, GOV-07) Operational State → EA-3 (GOV-08, GOV-09, GOV-11) Ops, Deployment, and Rules → EA-4 (GOV-12, GOV-13, GOV-14) Fleet Hygiene → out-of-plan addition (commits a6ba981..c2a2ca2 / 04d5e55)
AUDIENCE PERSONAS (STYLE.md §Audience Taxonomy): - Operator - Developer - Auditor - Incident Responder - Future Agent (Claude or successor)

gate.pass_criteria: README.md ≥ 150 substantive lines (comments/blank lines subtracted). Report both raw line count and substantive line count in completion.

gate.command: ls docs/governance/*.md | xargs -n 1 basename grep -oE '\[[^]]+\]\([a-z-]+\.md\)' docs/governance/README.md | sort -u
gate.pass_criteria: Every .md file in docs/governance/ (except README.md itself) must appear as a resolving link at least once in README.md. No extra links to nonexistent files except the phantom boot-sequence.md which is explicitly UNLINKED.

Output contains ONLY: - docs/governance/README.md - docs/ledger/<timestamp>_sprint9_ea5_governance-landing-page.md No src/, tests/, shared/, launcher/, services/ paths. No .py files. No edits to prior governance docs. L-15 + L-18 compliance MACHINE-VERIFIED by this gate.
Output: 0. Zero test-file overlap with Sprint 8's working set.

gate.command: grep -A 50 "## Audience Taxonomy Matrix" docs/governance/README.md | grep -c "^|"
gate.pass_criteria: ≥ 16 pipe-prefixed lines (1 header + 1 separator + 14 doc rows, 15 if fleet-hygiene is a distinct row — 16 expected). Matrix must have exactly 6 columns (Doc + 5 personas).

verification.step: 1. Run all quality gates (§8). Paste output verbatim into completion report.
2. Open README.md and click-walk each relative link by opening the target path from the README's directory. Any 404 = RED.
3. For each governance doc, open it, find its Audience section, and confirm the matrix row for that doc matches the Audience section's named personas. Mismatch = RED; fix the matrix (not the doc).
4. Confirm every ticket ID cited in Deferred Domains and Phantom References resolves via mcp__vikunja__get_task (no typos). Wrong ticket ID = RED.
5. ORACLE gate absolute: git diff main...HEAD --name-only shows exactly two files. If more, something was edited inadvertently — STOP and report before proceeding.
6. fleet-hygiene.md attribution check: confirm the provenance note in the Fleet Hygiene cluster cites the correct commit range. Use `git log --oneline -- docs/governance/fleet-hygiene.md` to verify.

commit_template.commit_message: [agent:ea_code] Sprint 9 EA-5: governance landing page (README synthesis)

completion_report.required_sections: - Summary (3-5 sentences) - Deliverables table (WI | Artifact | Lines | Notes) - Gate results (each of the six quality gates with command + output) - Inventory cross-check (every file in docs/governance/ mapped to its cluster + its matrix row) - Matrix construction notes (mapping rules used for near-synonym persona labels; any doc whose Audience section was ambiguous) - Cross-references (predecessor ledger, SDV §5.1 plan vs delivered, this prompt) - Follow-ups (any discovered defects in prior governance docs; fleet-hygiene.md out-of-plan flag for Sprint 9 SCR)

ea_prompt.mature_not_minimal:
The landing page is the front door to the governance class for years. Operators, auditors, and future agents will open it first. The 150-line floor is the minimum — the landing page earns its line count through the Audience Taxonomy Matrix (14 rows × substantive mapping) and the per-doc abstracts (one tight sentence per doc that names the doc's actual decision surface, not a bland "this doc covers X").
What polish looks like here: - Abstracts that name the specific decision or boundary the doc owns (e.g., "owns the 13-constraint TOML validation matrix" beats "covers runtime configuration") - Cluster intros that explain WHY the three docs cluster together (shared source files, shared failure mode, etc.) — traceable to the Sprint 9 SDV §7 clustering rationale - Matrix that actually reflects each doc's stated Audience section, not a generic "everyone reads everything" wash - Deferred-domains section that names the blocker (ISS-4 Pluton feasibility) and the owning ticket, so the reader knows WHO to ping and WHEN to re-check - Phantom reference (boot-sequence.md) called out honestly rather than silently omitted
What polish is NOT: - Padding the abstracts with filler - Re-explaining each doc's content (that's what the link is for) - Speculative discussion of docs that don't exist yet - Re-asserting normative claims that the linked docs own
